You go about, slave girl, just treading on the grain, Have you no fear, you wretch, your stomach thus to pain? 3. An imp; a devil; beldam; hellhag; jezebel. nigoṛā nāṭhā , Wom. n. m. 1. A single man; one who has no relatives. 2. A rogue; knave; rascal; villain.

Koī apnī āshnā se kar nāz kā jhapaṭṭā Kahtī hai haṅs-ke, 'kāfir, chuṭkī le yā nuhaṭṭā, Tum se to dil hamārā ab ho gayā hai khaṭṭā, Tum āj bhī na lāë raṅgvā merā du-paṭṭā!' Nazīr. def: s.m. Matrimony, marriage, nuptials (the most honourable kind of marriage, though in Bengal the term is applied to a left-handed marriage, such as that of a widow, which is considered disreputable)
more: n. m. Matrimony; marriage; nuptials; a legal marriage. Mard ke chār nikāh durust haiṅ . Mah. Prov. It is lawful for a man to have four wives. nikāh-i-bevagāṅ . G. G. Widow marriage. nikāh-paṛhāī , Marriage fees. nikāh paṛhānā , v. a. To tie the nuptial knot. nikāh paṛhā jānā , v. n. To be married. nikāh-i-sānī , n. m. A second marriage. nikāh k., yā nikāh meṅ lānā , v. n. To wed; take in marriage. nikāh-mutā . A marriage for a limited time in consideration of a present. nikāh-i-muvaqqat . A temporary marriage. The offspring of such marriages are legitimate. nikāh-nāmā , A marriage contract. nikāh'ī, nikāhtā , adj. Married (woman). arkān-i-nikāh , The essentials of a marriage, viz. the proposal and consent. sharāyat-i-nikāh , The conditions of marriage, viz. discretion, puberty, and freedom of the contracting parties.
s. m. Matrimony, marriage. (It is the most honourable kind of marriage, though in Bengal the term be only applicable to a secondary kind).
subs: (-kā), 'To read the marriage-ceremony' (over), to tie the nuptial knot

or nikāḥ-ě-muwaqqat, s.m. A temporary marriage (very common among Muhammadans, especially when detained from home: it is celebrated with certain forms, and not considered disreputable; and though the marriage itself is null and void in law, the offspring are legitimate)
(-ko), To take in marriage, to marry
s.m. Essentials of a marriage (viz. the proposal and consent)
s.f. The conditions of a marriage (viz. discretion, puberty, and freedom of the contracting parties).

ety: nikāl˚ = Prk. निक्काले(इ), or निक्काल(इ), fr. निक्काल = निक्कल्ह = निक्कड्ढ = S. निष्कृष्ट, p.p.p. of rt. निस्+कृष्; — or Prk. निक्काले(इ) = S. निष्कालय(ति), rt. निस्+कल्
def: v.t. (intens.) To drive out, eject, expel, &c.; — to take out; deduct; exclude, &c. (i.q. nikālnā)
more: v. a. S. निष्कासन or नष्कर्षण taking out; Pr. nikkāl , Pālī, nikkaḍḍh . See اينچنا کارَهنا 1—3., bāhar k. , and ījād k . 1. ( nikāl lenā ) To take out; subtract; deduct. 2. To pick out; select. 3. To bring out; give vent to. 4. ( nikāl denā ) To exclude; except. 5. To bring forth; hatch. 6. To bring or work out (a result); solve (a problem). Savāl nikālnā . 7. To thresh; beat out corn. Dānā nikālnā . 8. To extract; distil; milk. Hindū pūje gau. deotā, tīrath, barat asnānā he, Bachhṛā bāṅdh-ke dūdh nikāle, Rām kahāṅ se jānā he? Kabīr. The Hindu worships god and cow, makes pilgrimage, fasts, bathes enow, Binds fast the calf and milks the cow, how can he then Ram ever know? 9. To bring out, as a procession; exhibit; show. 10. To discover; find out; invent. 11. To strike a balance. 12. To remove; put away. 13. To renounce; cast off; drive away; discard. 14. To issue; publish. 15. To train or…

def: v.n. To be pulled or drawn out, to be taken out; to be expressed; to be extracted; to be extricated (from, -se); — to be deduced; — to be produced; to be invented; — to be hatched (eggs); — to be performed, or accomplished, or effected; — to be worked out, be solved; — to come out or forth, to issue, to emerge; to appear; to rise (as the sun); to germinate, to shoot; — to begin; — to come out or appear as a balance (to the credit of, -ke taʼīṅ, or against, -par, or -ke nām); — to result, prove, turn out; — to sally forth; — to find vent; to find utterance, to be uttered; — to go away, to depart, to proceed; to pass away (as life, or time); to secede; — to get out, to escape; to slink away, to give (one) the slip; to break loose (from, -se); — to go back (from a promise), to get out (of an engagement); to slip, to start (from its place); to spring (in these last senses, beginning with 'to go away,' the more com. form is nikal-jānā)
more: v. n. See nikālnā , v. a. 1. To issue; emerge; come out or forth. 2. To appear; come in sight. Rain baserā ho chukā, ab din niksā āë Chalo baṭāū des ko, khālī karo sarāë! Death. 3. To rise (as the Sun). Badlī kī dhūp jab nikle jab tez . Prov. A cloudy day, a hot sun. 4. To germinate; shoot. 5. ( nikal jānā ) To slink away; slip through one's fingers. 6. To pass away. Bahār ke din nikal gaë . 7. To spread abroad. 8. To break one's promise. Īqrār kar-ke āp merī jāṅ, nikal gaë . Rind. nikal bhāgnā , v. n. To run away; get off; break from; escape. nikal paṛnā , v. n. 1. To come out; to be drawn forth. 2. To drop; fall out. Ḍolī āī, ḍolī āī, mere man chāo, Ḍolī meṅ se nikal paṛā bhoṅkṛā bilāo! Prov. The doolie's come, the doolie's come, my heart goes pit-a-pat, From out the doolie issues forth, behold! an ugly cat! nikal jānā , v. n. To wear out ( chalnā , 14). nikal chalnā , v. n. 1. To esca…

n. m. H. ni not, khaṭnā to earn. An earn-nothing. So called from the shopman's practice of striking the first coin received during the day against the beam of his scales or his cash box. 1. Ni-khaṭṭū āve laṛtā, kamāū āve ḍartā . Prov. The nothing-earners fighting e'er, the bread-winner slinks home in fear. 2. Koī sūm, be-hayā, koī bolā ni-khaṭṭū hai, Beṭe ne jānā bāp to merā ni-khaṭṭū hai, Beṭi pukārtī hai ki 'bāvā ni-khaṭṭū hai, Bīvī ye dil meṅ kahtī hai, 'bhaṛvā ni-khaṭṭū hai,' Ākhir ni-khaṭṭū nām dharātī hai muflisī! Nazīr. A shameless miser dubbed by some, some say he nothing earns, To take him for an earn-nothing his son too quickly learns, His daughter too cries out 'my father is an earn-nothing,' His wife says to herself 'this whoreson is an earn-nothing.' In fine the name given him by poverty doth to him cling. 3. Rahā sukh to kināre, roṭī kapṛā bhī nahīṅ gat-kā, Ni-khaṭṭū thā yehī kyā, yā Ilāhī, merī qismat kā? Wom. Rāhat. Comfort aside, nor food nor clothes I get, To such an earn-nothing God linked my fate!
